steps.title: Identify the slope as rate of change
steps.content:
• The given equation $$H = 2.403L + 23.31$$ is linear in the form $$H = mL + b$$, where $$m$$ is the slope.
  • In a linear model, the slope $$m$$ tells how much the dependent variable (height) changes for a one‑unit increase in the independent variable (femur length).

steps.title: Interpret the slope in context
steps.content:
• Here the slope $$m = 2.403$$.
  • Therefore, each increase of 1 inch in femur length corresponds to an increase of 2.403 inches in estimated height.
  • That matches choice A — A is correct.
